rulings.Case Title: FELIX CHAN VS. ADAPTIVE MEDIAS, INC. FORMELY KNOWN AS MIMVI, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition By Felix Chan To Confirm Final Arbitration Decision
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 9, 2015, Line 2, PETITIONER FELIX CHAN Hearing On Petition By Felix Chan To Confirm Final Arbitration Decision. The motion to confirm the arbitration award is granted and the motion to vacate is denied as moot. AM did not show that the arbitrator failed to timely disclose a ground for disqualification of which the arbitrator was then aware. (See CCP 1286.2(6).) Moreover, the alleged relationship was too attenuated to require a disclosure even had the arbitrator been aware of it. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BRYNN K. STOLL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 9, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production pursuant to CCP 437c. Plaintiff alleges, inter alia, that the crosswalk was a dangerous condition of public property at par. 33 of her complaint. Defendant argues that the crosswalk was not a dangerous condition, but cites no evidence to support that assertion. In addition, the existence of a dangerous condition of public property is ordinarily a question of fact. In Bonanno v. Central Contra Costa Transit Authority (2003) 30 Cal.4th 139, the court explains that hazards present on adjoining property may create a dangerous condition of public property when users of the public property are necessarily exposed to those risks. Here, there is a disputed issue of material fact regarding whether defendant's crosswalk in combination with the other factors created a dangerous condition of public property. At paragraph 17 of his declaration, plaintiff's expert (Matthew Manharrez) explains that the skew angle of the crosswalk oriented plaintiff away from the approaching LRV and such a design decreased pedestrian visibility. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCp 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BRYNN K. STOLL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant City And County Of San Francisco And Andre Stephen Richardsons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 9, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ANDRE RICHARDSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The court denies the motion for summary adjudication. Defendant fails to maintain its initial burden of production per CCP 437c. Gov't Code secs. 830.4 and 830.8 do not bar this action because lack of signage/signals is merely one component of the alleged defective condition of public property. Defendant fails to show that the design immunity defense of Gov't Code sec. 830.6 applies. The Woo declaration does not sufficiently establish discretionary approval of the design, including Cal Trans' decision to use a crosswalk with a 50 degree angle skew. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the defective design caused the accident. There is also a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant had actual and/or constructive notice of the alleged defects. There was a prior similar accident at the intersection and defendant's 19th Ave. Corridor study noted some of the same cited defects. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KLATT, SUZANNE, AS SURVIVING HEIR OF PETER VS. RIGLER, JEFFERY, AS PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF THE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff Suzanne Klatts Appearance For Deposition And To Produce Requested Documents; Related Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday April 9, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT JEFFREY RIGLER'S (AS PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE) Motion To Compel Plaintiff Suzanne Klatt's Appearance For Deposition And To Produce Requested Documents; Related Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Dennis Hyde, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to hydelaw@pacbell.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BAKER ET AL VS. NINA MACKENZIE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 9, 2015, Line 4, Order To Show Cause. The order to show cause regarding preliminary injunction is denied. Petitioner MacKenzie fails to demonstrate a likelihood of prevailing on the merits. The weight of the disputed evidence indicates that the parties never entered into a partnership agreement. Petitioner is unlikely to prevail on her claims without being able to establish the existence of the partnership. Moreover, no showing is made that damages would be inadequate; there is no evidence the firm's goodwill is diminishing. rulings.Case Title: EDWARD REIDY VS. HARRY BONN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 9, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT HARRY BONN, BONN FINANCIAL AND INSURANCE SERVICES, METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, NEW ENGLAND LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY Defendants Petition To Compel Arbitration. The motion to compel arbitration is DENIED. The motion does not establish that the arbitration agreement was presented to plaintiff - he neither signed nor initialed the agreement and does not recall seeing it; defendant Bonn's declaration on this point is conclusory. Under California's law, which applies due to its public policies against adhesive contracts, the agreement is unconscionable and unenforceable on several grounds: (1) it is take-it-or-leave-it boilerplate; (2) the NASD arbitration rules were not attached or otherwise provided; (3) its comparison of "pre-arbitration discovery" with "court proceedings" is misleading; (4) it would effectively bar depositions in a case where they will likely be crucial to ascertainment of the truth; and (5) it seeks to bar claims after six years, though they may be intertwined with newer claims in a pattern of misconduct and not time-barred. Moreover, defendants have not proven that they are parties to the arbitration agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ERIKA MCCARTNEY VS. STARWEST BOTANICALS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Starwest Bontanicals, Inc.¿S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 9, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT STARWEST BOTANICALS INC. Defendant Starwest Bontanicals, Inc.'s Motion To Transfer Venue. The Motion to Transfer Venue is DENIED. San Francisco is a proper venue, as violations allegedly occurred here. As to convenience of witnesses for trial (if a trial occurs), defendant's Utah and New Mexico employees are effectively equidistant from San Francisco and Sacramento. For other employees, Sacramento is less than 100 miles from San Francisco.
